data.content: I need to download debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` using its BuildId, then use `addr2line` to resolve the addresses. Let me try fetching from Microsoft's symbol server.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -E 'binutils|llvm' 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading from Microsoft symbol server - ELF buildid format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-11cbabd44862440c86148a2b31ac6558/symbols"
mkdir -p "$SYMDIR"

# Try the standard Microsoft symbol server URL formats
for url in \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}.dbg"; do
  echo "Trying: $url"
  HTTP_CODE=$(curl -sS -o "$SYMDIR/response" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$url" 2>&1)
  echo "  HTTP: $HTTP_CODE, Size: $(wc -c < "$SYMDIR/response" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "200" ]; then
    file "$SYMDIR/response"
    break
  fi
done
arguments.description: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The key library is `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. The frames are at PC offsets:

- #01: 0x222098
- #02: 0x2241ac
- #03: 0x1abc80
- #04: 0x1abd14
- #05: 0x16dfa4
- #06: 0x16db6c


- #07: 0x16de04

To resolve these addresses to function names, I'd need the debug symbols for this specific build—either the unstripped `.so` file itself, a separate debug symbol file, or access to a symbol server. Microsoft typically publishes symbol packages for .NET runtime libraries, so the symbols might be available through their official channels using this BuildId.

I could try fetching the symbols directly from Microsoft's symbol server using standard tools like `dotnet symbol` or `curl`, following the ELF binary naming conventions they use for symbol lookups. available, or try downloading symbols directly from the Microsoft symbol server using the BuildId. Let me start by checking what tools I have access to and then attempt to fetch the debug symbols. I need to actually attempt downloading the symbols from that Microsoft server URL to see if it works, or find the separate symbol file if the binary doesn't have them embedded.

  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}.dbg"; do
  echo "Trying: $url"

toolArgs.command:
# Try downloading from Microsoft symbol server - ELF buildid format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-11cbabd44862440c86148a2b31ac6558/symbols"
mkdir -p "$SYMDIR"

# Try the standard Microsoft symbol server URL formats
for url in \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}.dbg"; do
  echo "Trying: $url"
  HTTP_CODE=$(curl -sS -o "$SYMDIR/response" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$url" 2>&1)
  echo "  HTTP: $HTTP_CODE, Size: $(wc -c < "$SYMDIR/response" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "200" ]; then
    file "$SYMDIR/response"
    break
  fi
done
toolArgs.description: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server
